data.title: Barton Gold Completes Pit Optimisation Drilling Campaign at Challenger Project
data.description:
Barton Gold (OTCQB: BGDFF) has completed an 8,065-metre reverse circulation drilling campaign at its Challenger project in South Australia.

Kennedy Drilling conducted the 30-day campaign for a total 112 holes at an average 269m per day, including 180m for diamond drilling pre-collar work.

The work focused on optimising open pit targets at Challenger Main, Challenger West, Challenger South-Southwest, and Challenger 3.

Barton plans to follow it with a 1,490m diamond drilling program to support the optimisation of pit designs and metallurgical work.

Mineralised Resource Conversion

Barton is targeting the conversion of easily accessible mineralisation within Challenger’s resource estimate of 10.6 million tonnes at 0.92 grams per tonne for 313,000 ounces gold to the Indicated category, advancing to ore reserves on completion of a definitive feasibility study (DFS).

The study is targeting an initial four-year Stage 1 ‘baseline’ operation using historical higher-grade material from the primary tailings storage facility (TSF1) and other near-surface materials without disturbing Challenger’s underground mine, its mineralisation, or its infrastructure access.

It aims to defer the technical risk and cost of underground operations to a future date (following the de-risking of Stage 1), providing the company with further time to refine its development plans.

Barton is also planning to refurbish the historic Central Gawler Mill adjacent to Challenger, to enhance the development optionality of regional assets including the Tarcoola and Wudinna gold projects and the high-grade Tolmer silver prospect.

Key Work Program

Managing director Alexander Scanlon said drilling at Challenger had proceeded to plan.

“We are pleased to have completed this key work program for our ongoing DFS and we thank Kennedy Drilling for the safe completion of this campaign despite challenging conditions from recent extreme rain events across outback South Australia,” he said.

“Challenger’s considerable on-pit and near-surface gold mineralisation […] provides an ideal pathway for an initial operation that underwrites a restart with a reduced risk profile while maximising development optionality for the Challenger underground mine, Tarcoola, Tolmer, and Wudinna.”

Barton expects assays from the campaign to be returned during the coming months.

data.title: Oil Rises on Lingering Risks of Prolonged U.S.-Iran War — Market Talk
data.description: Oil rises in early trade on lingering risks of a prolonged U.S.-Iran war. Crude-oil-price volatility is likely to persist on conflicting news flow about the war, Citi Research's Eric G. Lee says in a research report. "Our base case scenario assumes a continued conflict and disruption to the Strait [of Hormuz] for 4-6 weeks, before dealmaking starts in earnest," the energy strategist says. This could mean near-term oil prices as high as $120 per barrel at times, Lee adds. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are 1.1% higher at $91.35 per barrel; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.9% higher at $103.10 a barrel. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan's corporate service inflation perks up in February
data.description:
A key gauge of Japan's service-sector inflation rose 2.7% in February from a year earlier, data showed on Thursday, reinforcing the central bank's view that a tight labour market is pushing firms to pass rising costs on to consumers.

The Bank of Japan has stressed the need to see inflation durably hit its 2% target driven by rising wages and services prices, rather than higher raw material costs, to proceed with further interest rate hikes.

The increase in the services producer price index, which tracks the price companies charge each other for services, followed a 2.6% gain in January, BOJ data showed.

Prices rose for labour-intensive industries such as hotel and construction work, the data showed, a sign labour shortages were pushing up wages and service-sector inflation.

The BOJ ended a decade-long, massive stimulus programme in 2024 and in December raised short-term interest rates to 0.75% on the view Japan was on the cusp of durably meeting its 2% inflation target.

With consumer inflation having exceeded its 2% target for nearly four years, the central bank has signaled its readiness to keep hiking borrowing costs if prices continue to rise steadily accompanied by higher wages.

data.title: South Korea Set to Post Stronger Export Growth in March, Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: South Korea is set to post stronger export growth in March, largely driven by brisk semiconductor shipments. The median forecast from a Wall Street Journal poll of nine economists is for a 42.9% on-year increase in shipments overseas, following a revised 28.7% rise in February. Imports are likely to rise 19.6% on year in March, resulting in a $19.4 billion trade surplus--wider than the prior month's revised $15.38 billion surplus, the poll shows. Higher chip and oil prices, along with one more working day than a year earlier, are expected to have boosted trade for the month, Citigroup economist Jin-Wook says. The trade data are due next Wednesday. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: RH Officer Sold Shares Worth Over $905K

The transactions show weighted average selling prices of $127.9594, $128.7428, $129.8314 and $130.7354 per share across four sales. The total sale amount reported is $905,950. data.title: Air New Zealand's Risk-Reward Skewed to the Downside — Market Talk
data.description: Rising fuel prices have skewed Air New Zealand's risk-reward to the downside, Forsyth Barr says. It retains an underperform call and cuts its price target by 30% to NZ$0.35/share. "Air New Zealand's situation was already challenged before the Middle Eastern conflict began," analyst Andy Bowley says. "Losses have been exacerbated by its exposure to the unhedged jet fuel crack spread, creating additional balance sheet pressure." Air New Zealand was already above the top end of its target gearing range in 1H. Forsyth Barr notes Air New Zealand is trading on a multiple of 0.86x trailing net tangible assets. "Such a discount may appear attractive," it says. "However, Air New Zealand has traded as low as 0.39x NTA historically." Air New Zealand is up 1.2% at NZ$0.44. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: What did jury decide in social media case against Meta and Google?
data.description:
A Los Angeles jury found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable on Wednesday in a landmark youth social media addiction trial.

Here are the details of the jury's verdict:

META, GOOGLE OWE DAMAGES

Meta and Google owe $3 million in compensatory damages and $3 million in punitive damages. Meta will be liable for 70% of the total and Google for 30%.

META WAS NEGLIGENT

The jury found Meta was negligent in designing or operating Instagram.

META'S CONDUCT DEEMED 'SUBSTANTIAL' FACTOR IN WOMAN'S HARM

The jury found that Meta's negligence was a "substantial factor" in harming the 20-year-old plaintiff. The plaintiff said earlier in the legal proceeding her Instagram use fueled her mental health struggles.

META DID NOT ADEQUATELY WARN USERS

The jury found Meta liable for failing to adequately warn users about the dangers of using Instagram.

GOOGLE WAS NEGLIGENT

The jury found Google was negligent in designing or operating YouTube.

GOOGLE'S CONDUCT DEEMED 'SUBSTANTIAL' FACTOR IN WOMAN'S HARM

The jury found Google's negligence was a "substantial factor" in harming the 20-year-old plaintiff. The plaintiff has said YouTube's features contributed to her depression and anxiety.

GOOGLE DID NOT ADEQUATELY WARN USERS

The jury found Google liable for failing to adequately warn users about the dangers of using YouTube.

data.title: Worst Likely Over for China Mengniu Dairy — Market Talk
data.description: China Mengniu Dairy's earnings are likely to begin recovering this year as margins expand on a cyclical rebound in raw milk prices and improved operating leverage, Citi analyst Xiaopo Wei says in a note. Core net profit in 2025 fell 13% but beat Citi's forecast. The bank says upside came from stronger operating cash flow, up 5%, and a 2% increase in dividend per share despite the profit decline. Mengniu also announced a three-year plan to raise dividends while maintaining its share-repurchase pace. Citi maintains its buy rating with a target price of HK$21.10. (venkat.pr@wsj.com)

data.title: Key facts: XRP pilots RLUSD, NYSE note; SEC/Clarity, flows, suit, CTO
data.description:
• XRPUSD traders monitor SEC classification updates and CLARITY Act progress, plus shifting macro conditions that could affect liquidity and volatility.1
  • Spot inflows into XRP surged. If inflows match price, XRP could test nearby resistance and the 50-period EMA; if not, inflows may be absorbed as sell-side liquidity.2
  • NYSE MoU with Securitize mentioned XRP as a potential neutral bridge asset for fast cross-border transfers, citing Ripple tools like On-Demand Liquidity (ODL) and RLUSD.3
  • Ripple and Unloq pilot RLUSD stablecoin payments on the XRP Ledger for automated cross‑border trade settlements; RLUSD triggers on shipment verification to demonstrate tokenized settlement flows.4
  • Ripple CTO David Schwartz said Ripple won’t subsidize or offer discounts to boost XRP adoption, calls such moves risky price manipulation, and focuses on lowering cross-border payment friction.5
  • Apex Crypto analyst Jesse said the XRP lawsuit may have been used to pressure retail holders and affect XRPUSD price dynamics, tied to timing of institutional deals and ecosystem growth.6

data.title: Key facts: Solana reclaims $90; bullish longs exceed 3:1, OI $5.1B
data.description:
• SOLUSD reclaimed above $90 after support near $85 and breach of $88 trendline; near-term resistance at $92.80 and $95, targets at $102 and $112 if cleared; supports $88.40, $85, 100‑hour MA.1
  • Solana (SOLUSD) saw long/short ratios above 3:1 on some exchanges. Aggregate open interest held near $5.1B with little change, indicating strong long positioning and flat derivatives exposure.2

data.title: Key facts: ETH near $2,180; exch supply 2016 low; BitMine +65k, CB dip
data.description:
• Ethereum (ETHUSD) faces near-term resistance near $2,180. Key upside targets: $2,200, 50% retracement of $2,385–$2,025, then resistance zones at $2,250, $2,300 and $2,345–$2,365.1
  • Ethereum supply on exchanges hit a 2016 low as Binance outflows and staking increased. ETH traded near $2,180 after a $2,198 peak, below key moving averages; $2,500 cited as reclaim level.2
  • BitMine added 65,341 ETH from March 16–22, raising holdings to ~4.66M ETH (~$10B), about 3.86% of circulating supply; needs roughly $359M more to hit 4.0%.34
  • Ethereum (ETHUSD) traded at a slight Coinbase discount, Coinbase premium ~-0.0149 over 24 hours, signaling heavier sell pressure on Coinbase versus Binance while overall ETH price held steady.5

data.title: Key facts: Dogecoin Eyes $0.10 Break; 4-hr Triangle Implies 29% Move
data.description:
• Dogecoin (DOGEUSD) eyes break above $0.098, key resistance at $0.10. Short-term sentiment bullish; traders watching for volume spike and any utility updates.1
  • On the 4-hour chart, Dogecoin (DOGEUSD) forms a descending triangle. Measured move from the pattern’s height suggests a potential ~29% price swing if a breakout happens.2

data.title: Key facts: Bitcoin mild inflows, weaker whales, large holdings
data.description:
• Since late February BTCUSD saw mild net ETF inflows. Long-term holders are adding coins to the market. Perpetual swap open interest near yearly lows with negative funding; CME futures OI flat.1
  • Bhutan’s gov-linked wallet moved ~519.7 BTC (~$36.7m) to two addresses, one linked to QCP Capital. Prior March outflows: ~72m and ~$11.8–$12m. Remaining holdings: 4,453 BTC (~$315m).23
  • BTCUSD large transfers fell: daily on-chain transfers >$100k down to 6,417 and >$1M to 1,485, signaling reduced whale activity as traders await macro and policy clarity.4
  • On-chain data: unrealized losses among BTC holders are rising but below past capitulation levels; supply is stacked above current BTCUSD price and must be absorbed for a breakout from the range.5
  • Capriole Investment’s Bitcoin Macro Index fell to -1.37, a reading the firm associates with prior bear-cycle conditions for BTCUSD; the index value and its historical context were the facts reported.6
  • Forensic tracking linked a recovered wallet to a cluster tied to about 5,500 BTC (~$391M), connecting on-chain addresses and noting cross‑border cooperation in the investigation.7
  • Analyst gave BTCUSD key levels: likely bear-market bottom near $35,000, alternate low ~$42,000, possible local top about $79,000; BTC was trading near $71,200 at report time.8
  • CardoneCapital holds about $70M in Bitcoin and aims to reach 10,000 BTC by year-end, funding purchases with luxury rental income; Cardone says fair BTC value is near $280,000 vs ~ $70,000 now.9
  • Analyst ‘Doctor Profit’ holds a large short on BTCUSD around $115k–$125k, stays bearish, and may add shorts near $79k–$84k using up to 5x leverage.10
